parameters.design: 6 budgets x 4 base selections + 2 modified selections x 2 budgets

prediction_outcome: Scenario B (distributed positional bias) best matches results. Early bias is structural, not sink-dominated. Sink fraction only 11-12% vs predicted >50% for Scenario A. Both partial fixes help but neither fully rescues true H2O.
key_finding: True H2O selects early positions (Q1=39-40%, mean_pos=0.32) while K-norm selects middle-late (mean_pos=0.52-0.56). The bias extends far beyond attention sinks (only 11-12%). Budget crossover at 40% on Llama (true_h2o = k_norm = 76.5%). At 60%: true_h2o=100% vs k_norm=82.4%. Random > BOTH at 25-33% on Llama. Sink-excluded H2O +11.8pp on Llama; late-layer H2O +13.3pp on Qwen. 'Recent' 100% at ALL 6 budgets. Anti-correlation rho=-0.435 replicates.
evidence_impact: Added findings #81-87. Answered open questions 5b (why anti-correlation) and 5c (crossover budget). Updated compression synthesis section.
next_planned: Test combined K-norm + recency hybrid; investigate why random beats biased selection on Llama; cross-model position profiles on Phi/Mistral

null_space_existence.status: CONFIRMED in K-space (p=0.013). Narrow but real. Qwen-specific.

spatial_structure.status: DECISIVELY DISCONFIRMED for selectivity. HEAD-LEVEL CONCENTRATION decisive on Qwen, weak on Llama.

positional_dissociation.status: Complete. Head x position interaction is encoding-dependent.

geometric_dissociation.status: K > V UNIVERSAL at per-head level: 16/16 heads across 2 models.

head_level_analysis.status: COMPLETE: Two-regime on Qwen (decisive), near-collapse on Llama.

natural_usage.status: ADVANCED: 4 eviction benchmarks complete. Position profiles mapped. True H2O = early-biased (Q1=40%); K-norm = late-biased. Crossover at 40% on Llama. Random > biased at low budgets. 'Recent' 100% at ALL 6 budgets. Sink-excluded & late-layer H2O partially rescue.
